title: Strategic interactions for carbon emissions in Chinese cities are influenced by mayors

data_scope.headline_city_count: not stated in public abstract/metadata; exact N in Supplementary data description
data_scope.key_quantitative_finding: 1% decrease in neighboring cities' carbon intensity associated with 0.792% reduction in reference city
data_scope.decision_maker_attributes: mayor education, age, academic major (science vs non-science), hometown/local ties

core_conclusion: Chinese cities reduce carbon intensity through strategic peer interaction rather than in isolation: a spatial Durbin model on 2000-2019 city panel data shows a 1% drop in neighboring cities' carbon intensity is associated with a 0.792% drop in the reference city. The effect is stronger for mayors who are more educated, younger, science-trained, and working in their hometowns, and follows an 'imitation competition' pattern.
why_it_matters: Breaks the 'city as island' assumption by showing decarbonization is networked and peer-driven; reintroduces the individual decision-maker (mayor attributes) as a behavioral micro-foundation; identifies an imitation-competition diffusion mechanism that gives central planners low-cost leverage (demonstration cities, cadre profiles) for China's dual-carbon goals.
why_nature: Novel cross-disciplinary framing bridging environmental economics, political economy of bureaucracy, and behavioral psychology; mechanism with direct climate-policy stakes; transferable lens (strategic interaction among officials) beyond carbon and beyond China; strongly policy-translatable to cadre evaluation and regional climate governance.
added_value_to_my_research: Learned multiplex city interaction graph replacing fixed geographic weight matrix -> reusable who-imitates-whom GeoAI operator
Policy-copying vs official-transfer decomposition -> identification template for human-mediated diffusion of healthcare/telehealth practice
Explainable ML (SHAP) over the SDM backbone -> interpretable attribution of mayor-attribute interactions, reused for accessibility drivers
Carbon-imitation network coupled to exposure-mortality -> spillovers of governance become spillovers of population health
Convergence/divergence equity diagnostic over the interaction graph -> efficiency-vs-equity audit of networked policy diffusion (EV-charging siting)
